about.intro: Technical Theatre student specialising in sound design — moving from stage to desk, and loving every second of it.
paragraphs: I'm Louis, a BOA Stage & Screen Technical Theatre student in Year 12. My theatre journey began on stage, performing in productions such as Popstars' 90s Musical (2019), Oklahoma! (2020), Battle of Boat (2022), Bugsy Malone (2023), and The Wizard of Oz (2024). Over time I discovered my passion lay behind the scenes — especially in sound.

In 2025 I moved fully into sound with SOSage and Cookie Theatre Company, leading audio for live performances: programming and running sound effects, balancing levels, supporting rehearsals, and troubleshooting in real time.

Alongside this, I've built more than four years of experience at Alderbrook School, working extensively with the Behringer X32 digital sound desk. I've mixed live audio for We Will Rock You, Grease, The Wizard of Oz, High School Musical, and National Theatre Connections at the Royal & Derngate, Northampton.

Proficient in QLab 5 — building and running cue stacks, triggering effects, and synchronising sound with other technical elements, including managing inputs and outputs, EQ, and solving problems under pressure.

skills.title: Communication
skills.desc: Comfortable explaining ideas, giving direction, and working with different teams on and off stage.

skills.title: Strong Teamwork
skills.desc: Used to collaborating with actors, directors, and tech crews to keep everything running smoothly.

skills.title: Problem Solving
skills.desc: Able to think fast and sort issues during live shows or tight turnarounds.

skills.title: Technical Ability
skills.desc: Deep understanding of modern computer systems and professional audio/lighting hardware.

skills.title: Time Management
skills.desc: Always prepared, organised, and on schedule for rehearsals and events.

skills.title: Creative
skills.desc: Able to shape ideas, adapt quickly, and bring fresh solutions when plans change.

performance.intro: My theatre journey began on stage — these roles gave me the empathy to work alongside performers as a sound operator and technician.

cv.profile: Enthusiastic and technically adept theatre technician with practical experience in sound design, live audio mixing, and stage production. Skilled in supporting live performances through precision, teamwork, and problem solving under pressure. Adept at operating industry-standard equipment and working collaboratively with production teams to deliver high-quality theatrical experiences.

bullets: Designed the soundscape for the production.
Created a time and location established soundscape.
Liaised with the director to perfect the design.

experience.title: Sound Designer & QLab Operator
bullets: Took notes during rehearsals to brief the technical team effectively.
Managed QLab for all rehearsals and shows, covering all sound effects.
Led as sound designer, taking responsibility for the overall soundscape.
Worked closely with the stage management team.

noda.intro: Independent show reviews from the National Operatic and Dramatic Association — the UK's largest amateur theatre organisation.

reviews.title: The Wizard of Oz
reviews.quote:
Great support also came from Halle Kwarteng and Louis Haywood as Aunt Em and Uncle Henry.

The entire company worked well together with some lovely singing and acting.

reviews.title: Bugsy Malone
reviews.venue: Sosage Factory · The Core Theatre, Solihull
reviews.quote:
The battle between Fat Sam (Louis Haywood) and Dandy Dan's gang was excellent.

Our future is secure with this society full of talented members.

reviews.title: Zombie Prom
reviews.quote:
The singing both principals and chorus could be heard.

Congratulations to the cast and crew… the very talented members of SOSage Factory did it proud.

reviews.title: SpongeBob The Musical
reviews.venue: Sosage Factory · The Core Theatre, Solihull

reviews.title: Sosage Does It Again
reviews.quote:
The sound was good and not too loud so that every word could be heard.

Another sell out performance from this group of very talented young people.

reviews.title: The Battle of the Boat
reviews.quote:
The remaining cast and chorus gave their all in this wonderful production.

Two hours of emotive drama that was heart-warming, funny and really entertaining.
